notes: Contains older sprints moved verbatim from the rolling log to reduce context bloat.
This file is append-only: do not rewrite old rounds.

sprint_meta.notes: WK7: UI overhaul — window/display modes, ESC menu, castle build catalog, and Majesty-quality UI benchmark pass.

round_meta.visibility: WK7 kickoff: only send to active agents. Consult-only agents stand down unless pinged.

wk7_focus: UI polish to Majesty standard (quality uplift, not a clone).
Window/display mode options (fullscreen/borderless/windowed) exposed in ESC menu → Graphics.
ESC opens a centered pause/settings menu; world input is blocked while open.
Castle-driven Build Catalog window with building thumbnails + click-to-place.

locked_decisions.bottom_bar_scope: Keep a slim always-visible bottom bar for core actions; ESC menu is primarily settings/options.
locked_decisions.borderless_drag_behavior: In fullscreen windowed (borderless), click+hold+drag on the top bar switches to windowed and live-drags with the cursor (best-effort).
locked_decisions.drag_degradation_policy: If live-drag cannot be implemented on the platform/runtime, degrade to switching to windowed at drag start and show a brief HUD message explaining the limitation.

pm_integration_order: Agent 03: add/solidify runtime display-mode switching API in engine (apply_display_settings + settings model) + best-effort borderless top-bar drag → windowed live-drag.
Agent 08: implement ESC menu modal + Graphics/Audio/Controls pages using Agent 03 API; block world input while menu is open; wire ESC behavior to show menu.
Agent 14: define audio settings semantics (master volume; optional split) and ensure UI hooks do not violate the visibility-gated audibility rule.
Agent 08: implement castle-driven Build Catalog panel (center screen) + click-to-place; add thumbnails/fallback visuals.
Agent 08: run UI benchmark vs Majesty reference and propose/implement highest-value polish deltas that fit sprint scope.
Agent 11: verify gates + manual checklist for window modes + ESC menu + build catalog; file bugs as needed.

asks: Implement runtime display switching: fullscreen, borderless (NOFRAME), windowed (RESIZABLE) and persist user prefs (UI-only, non-sim).
Add a single engine entrypoint like apply_display_settings() so UI can request a mode change safely.
Implement borderless top-bar click+drag to switch to windowed and live-drag with cursor using pygame._sdl2 if available; otherwise degrade per policy with a HUD message.
Expose the active display mode + window size in game_state so Agent 08 can render correct labels in the ESC Graphics menu.
Keep determinism unaffected: settings are UI-only; do not introduce wall-clock time into sim paths.
acceptance_criteria: User can switch among fullscreen/borderless/windowed via ESC → Graphics and see changes apply immediately.
In borderless: click+hold+drag in top bar switches to windowed; window follows cursor (best-effort).
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS
suggested_files: config.py (defaults only; no UI state stored here long-term)

asks: Implement ESC menu modal (center screen): Resume, Graphics, Audio, Controls, Quit.
Route Esc to open/close the menu (not just pause). While menu open, block world input; game is paused.
Graphics page: show display mode options (fullscreen/borderless/windowed), call Agent 03 apply_display_settings().
Audio page: master volume slider (coordinate with Agent 14).
Controls page: read-only list of keybinds (rebinding UI is optional if time permits).

Castle-driven Build Catalog: selecting castle shows a Build buildings action; clicking opens centered catalog with thumbnails/name/cost/hotkey + disabled reasons; clicking a building closes panel and enters placement (must call engine.select_building_for_placement()).
UI benchmark vs Majesty reference: capture screenshots, list deltas, then implement the top-impact improvements that fit the sprint.
acceptance_criteria: Esc opens centered menu; Resume closes; world input is blocked while open; game is paused while menu is open.
Graphics/Audio/Controls pages render without clutter and remain readable at 1920x1080.
Castle → Build buildings opens the catalog; clicking an item enters placement and closes the catalog.
Before/after screenshot set + delta list recorded in Agent 08 log.
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

pm_bug_tickets.title: Boot crash: PauseMenu constructed before audio_system (AttributeError: GameEngine has no attribute audio_system)
repro_steps: Run: python main.py (or python main.py --no-llm / --provider mock)
Observe crash during GameEngine.__init__
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Game boots successfully; ESC menu can reference AudioSystem safely.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Crash: AttributeError: 'GameEngine' object has no attribute 'audio_system' when constructing PauseMenu.
evidence: Trace shows: game/engine.py calls PauseMenu(... audio_system=self.audio_system) before self.audio_system exists.
suspected_area: game/engine.py (GameEngine.__init__ initialization order)
game/ui/pause_menu.py (expects audio_system reference)
constraints: Keep settings/UI non-authoritative (no sim-state changes).
Keep determinism unchanged.
Avoid headless/dummy driver regressions if possible.
proposed_fix: Reorder GameEngine initialization so self.audio_system is created before PauseMenu is constructed.
If any UI construction needs window_width/height, ensure those are set before allocating surfaces (avoid init-order crashes).
Keep the change minimal and localized to engine init ordering.
acceptance_test: python main.py --no-llm boots to a running window (no crash)
python main.py --provider mock boots to a running window (no crash)
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

risk_notes: This is a constructor-order bug; fixing it should not change gameplay behavior, but it is a blocker for any manual smoke.
If additional headless boot issues exist (SDL dummy), handle them without changing sim determinism.
consult: 11_QA_TestEngineering_Lead to re-run qa_smoke and confirm boot

pm_bug_tickets.title: UI click hitboxes are vertically offset (need to click above control)
repro_steps: Run: python main.py
Try clicking the Build Buildings / build catalog controls (and other UI controls)
Observe click registers only when clicking significantly above the visible control
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Click target matches the visible control bounds.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Click registers offset upward by ~100–200px.
suspected_area: Coordinate space mismatch between panel-local drawing and screen-space Rect hit-tests
Modal panels caching stale screen sizes after display mode changes
constraints: UI-only; do not change sim determinism.
Avoid per-frame allocations; preserve caching where possible.
proposed_fix: Audit clickable rects in build-related UI and enforce consistent coordinate-space rules (panel-local vs screen-space).
Add explicit on_resize(w,h) hooks for PauseMenu / BuildCatalogPanel / HUD (as needed) and call them from apply_display_settings() after pygame.set_mode.
acceptance_test: Manual: click targets align in fullscreen/borderless/windowed
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

pm_bug_tickets.title: Graphics menu display mode switching appears to do nothing (stays fullscreen)
repro_steps: Run: python main.py
ESC → Graphics → choose Windowed or Borderless
Observe screen flicker/attempt but ends up fullscreen anyway
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Windowed is obviously windowed (decorated/resizable); borderless is fullscreen-windowed; fullscreen is exclusive fullscreen.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Remains fullscreen regardless of selection (or looks fullscreen).
suspected_area: Windowed defaults to desktop size so it looks fullscreen
SDL/pygame flags not clearing properly between modes on Windows
UI not receiving resized screen_w/h after mode change (layout/hitboxes stale)
constraints: UI/system-only; do not change sim determinism.
Avoid new persistent settings storage in BuildA unless trivial.
proposed_fix: Make windowed mode default clearly smaller (e.g., 1280×720) unless prior windowed size exists.
Ensure mode switching clears/sets flags appropriately on Windows and triggers UI resize propagation.
Optional: minimal HUD toast showing the active mode + resolution after switching.
acceptance_test: Manual: each mode is distinct and stable on Windows
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

pm_bug_tickets.title: Windowed mode window is stuck top-left and cannot be moved
repro_steps: Run: python main.py
ESC → Graphics → switch to Windowed
Try dragging the window by the title bar around the desktop
pm_bug_tickets.expected: In windowed mode, the OS-decorated window can be dragged/moved normally.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Window appears locked to the top-left corner and cannot be moved.
suspected_area: pygame/SDL window position handling when switching modes
Mode switch code may be forcing position (0,0) or reapplying a fixed position each frame
Possible use of borderless flags or SDL2 window APIs even in windowed mode
constraints: UI/system-only; do not change sim determinism.
Avoid brittle platform-specific hacks; prefer SDL/pygame-supported window positioning.
proposed_fix: Ensure windowed mode uses RESIZABLE with OS decorations (no NOFRAME) and does not forcibly set window position after switching.
If we intentionally set a position during mode switch, set it ONCE (centered) and then stop touching it.
If using pygame._sdl2 Window, verify we are not pinning the position or recreating the window each frame.
acceptance_test: Manual (Windows): after switching to windowed, user can drag/move the window anywhere.
Switching between modes preserves move-ability in windowed.
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

pm_bug_tickets.title: World/play area does not re-expand when switching Windowed → Fullscreen
repro_steps: Run: python main.py
ESC → Graphics → Windowed (choose a smaller window)
ESC → Graphics → Fullscreen
Observe UI expands but world/play area remains shrunk/incorrectly scaled
pm_bug_tickets.expected: After switching to fullscreen/borderless, world/play area and camera viewport use the new display size.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: World/play area stays at the previous smaller size despite UI expanding.
suspected_area: game/engine.py render pipeline: scaled surface/view surface sizing not recomputed on set_mode
Camera/viewport clamping uses stale window_width/height
Cached surfaces tied to old size not invalidated on display mode switch
constraints: UI/system-only; no sim determinism changes.
Keep perf-safe: recompute only on mode change, not per-frame.
proposed_fix: On apply_display_settings: recompute any world-render target surfaces and scaling factors for the new screen size.
Ensure window_width/height and any HUD-reserved margins are updated before rendering/world viewport calculations.
If any cached surfaces depend on size, invalidate/rebuild them on resize.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Boot crash: apply_display_settings -> clamp_camera uses self.zoom before initialization (AttributeError: zoom)
evidence: Trace: game/engine.py __init__ calls apply_display_settings(); apply_display_settings calls clamp_camera(); clamp_camera reads self.zoom but GameEngine has no attribute zoom yet.
repro_steps: Run: python main.py
Observe crash during GameEngine.__init__ with AttributeError: 'GameEngine' object has no attribute 'zoom'
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Game boots successfully in all display modes; clamp_camera can always read zoom safely.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Crash on boot: AttributeError for self.zoom.
suspected_area: game/engine.py initialization order for zoom/window sizing/camera
apply_display_settings called too early in __init__ before zoom is set
constraints: No simulation determinism changes; UI/system-only.
Keep fix minimal and robust (no reliance on optional attributes).
proposed_fix: Initialize self.zoom (and any related camera vars used by clamp_camera) before calling apply_display_settings in __init__.
Alternatively, make clamp_camera resilient: treat missing zoom as 1.0 and avoid AttributeError.
Re-run headless safety if applicable and ensure qa_smoke remains PASS.
acceptance_test: python main.py boots (no crash)
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

pm_decisions.tier_color_language: Low=gray, Med=gold, High=green (value-contrast first; must remain readable under fog-of-war).

P0 tech blocker: ensure render/camera/scaling paths do not assume WINDOW_WIDTH/WINDOW_HEIGHT when window resolution differs due to fallback. (Engine currently sets `self.window_width/height`, but many downstream uses still reference constants; this can break smaller-display fallback.)
Audit trail gap: Agent 03 did not log a wk3_r2 entry even though display-mode code appears landed. We need a retro-log for traceability.
integration_risks: Fallback resolution risk: `pygame.transform.scale(..., (WINDOW_WIDTH, WINDOW_HEIGHT), self._scaled_surface)` and camera edge logic may mismatch the actual window size when fallback < 1920x1080. Must use `self.window_width/self.window_height` (or derive from `self.screen.get_width/height`) consistently.
Perf risk: legacy `building_panel.py` and `debug_panel.py` allocate surfaces per render; keep behind debug toggles or schedule a caching pass if they remain frequently visible.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Boot crash: UITheme dataclass(slots=True) cannot set font_* attributes in __post_init__
repro_steps: Run: python main.py (or python main.py --no-llm / --provider mock)
Observe crash during GameEngine init when HUD creates UITheme
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Game boots successfully; UITheme initializes fonts safely.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Crash: AttributeError: 'UITheme' object has no attribute 'font_title' and no __dict__ for setting new attributes
evidence: Trace points to game/ui/theme.py: UITheme.__post_init__ assigning self.font_title/body/small on a slots dataclass without declaring those fields.
suspected_area: game/ui/theme.py (UITheme dataclass)
game/ui/hud.py (constructs UITheme in HUD.__init__)
constraints: Keep behavior deterministic and perf-safe.
Prefer keeping slots=True; declare font fields properly (init=False) rather than removing slots unless necessary.
proposed_fix: In UITheme dataclass, add fields: font_title/font_body/font_small (e.g., pygame.font.Font | None) with init=False default=None, then assign in __post_init__.
Alternative: remove slots=True (less preferred).
acceptance_test: python main.py --no-llm boots to a running window (no crash)
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick remains PASS
resolution_summary: Agent 08 kept `@dataclass(slots=True)` and declared `font_title/font_body/font_small` as dataclass fields (init=False, default=None), then assigned them in `UITheme.__post_init__`.
Boot verified in `--no-llm` and `--provider mock` and `python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick` PASS per Agent 08 log (wk3_r4).
resolution_evidence: Code: game/ui/theme.py declares the font fields using dataclasses.field(...) and assigns in __post_init__.

gates.fps_sanity: Agent 10 judgement: FPS with three prefabs in view within ~30% of WK29 baseline.

pm_feature_requests.title: Generalize prefab loader in ursina_renderer.py (default-on + opt-out)
acceptance: Renderer resolves prefab path by building_type (e.g. assets/prefabs/buildings/<building_type>_v1.json) and instantiates via the existing _load_prefab_instance path when the file exists.
Prefabs load by default; KINGDOM_URSINA_PREFAB_TEST=0 (explicit zero) forces legacy render path for all buildings.

Per-piece classifier (tools.model_viewer_kenney._apply_gltf_color_and_shading) still applied at instantiation; if classifier is relocated to game/graphics/, tools/model_viewer_kenney.py and tools/model_assembler_kenney.py imports updated in same PR.
Cache per sim building id retained (no per-frame reloads).
No wall-clock time; no unseeded RNG; determinism_guard PASS.
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS.
Agent 03 log documents the building_type -> prefab path resolution strategy.

pm_feature_requests.title: Prefab-aware asset manifest + validator
acceptance: tools/assets_manifest.json gains a prefabs.buildings section. REQUIRED entries: peasant_house_small_v1, castle_v1, warrior_guild_v1, ranger_guild_v1, rogue_guild_v1. OPTIONAL entry: wizard_guild_v1.

tools/validate_assets.py checks each prefab JSON: exists, parses, has required keys (prefab_id, building_type, footprint_tiles, ground_anchor_y, pieces, attribution), every model path resolves under assets/models/, attribution non-empty.
Missing OPTIONAL prefab emits WARN (not ERROR); missing REQUIRED prefab or malformed JSON emits ERROR.
python tools/validate_assets.py --report exit 0 when all five required prefabs are present and well-formed (regardless of whether wizard_guild_v1 exists).

pm_agent_prompts.15: WK30 R1 (ModelAssembler — HIGH): Kitbash four new prefabs with Jaimie (Wizard Guild optional stretch). (4) `--new --prefab-id rogue_guild_v1` — <=12 pieces, steel/gray lower-profile silhouette, distinct from Warrior AND Ranger. (5, OPTIONAL) `--new --prefab-id wizard_guild_v1` — <=14 pieces, purple accents, tower motif. Only if energy remains after the core four; not a sprint blocker. Fill attribution arrays; update assets/ATTRIBUTION.md if any new pack is used. After Agent 03 lands the generalized loader, confirm each prefab renders in-game via `python main.py --renderer ursina` (NO env var needed — default-on). Round-trip each via `--open <prefab_id>`. Preserve the per-building-id cache. Keep per-piece classifier (tools.model_viewer_kenney._apply_gltf_color_and_shading). pm_agent_prompts.12: WK30 R1 (Tools Director — MEDIUM): Teach the asset pipeline about prefabs. (a) Add a `prefabs.buildings` section to tools/assets_manifest.json. REQUIRED entries: `peasant_house_small_v1`, `castle_v1`, `warrior_guild_v1`, `ranger_guild_v1`, `rogue_guild_v1`. OPTIONAL entry: `wizard_guild_v1`. (b) Extend tools/validate_assets.py: per prefab — file exists, JSON parses, required keys present (prefab_id / building_type / footprint_tiles / ground_anchor_y / pieces / attribution), every piece `model` path resolves under assets/models/, attribution non-empty. Tune severity: missing OPTIONAL prefab = WARN, missing REQUIRED / malformed = ERROR. `python tools/validate_assets.py --report` must exit 0 once the five REQUIRED prefabs are in place (regardless of whether wizard_guild_v1 ships). Log one bullet per building: `<type> | config footprint | prefab footprint | OK / mismatch`. If mismatch, file back to Agent 15 in this hub for a prefab shrink. Do NOT edit config.py. pm_bug_tickets.title: Camera regression: starts far from castle (near-black screen) after Agent 03 WK32 commit
repro_steps: git log shows a8a9864 (Agent 03 WK32 commit) last to touch game/graphics/ursina_app.py
python main.py --renderer ursina

Was working before Agent 03's WK32 commit; was also working in PM's emergency ursina_app.py fix (which Agent 03 was supposed to adopt).
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Near-black screen; tiny terrain sliver visible at bottom; grass dots only. Castle not in frame. Ursina EMA FPS showing 1 (near-frozen render).
pm_bug_tickets.pm_diagnosis_hints: Agent 03 made 124 line changes to ursina_app.py in a8a9864. Likely failure modes: (A) EditorCamera pivot position (ec.position = Vec3(cx,0,cz)) ended up at (0,0,0) because castle lookup failed — engine.buildings is populated AFTER _setup_ursina_camera_for_castle is called, so the castle search returns None and cx/cz fall back to _map_center_xz which could be computed incorrectly before the world is built; (B) camera.position local offset signs are wrong (e.g. positive Z pushes camera through the terrain instead of behind it); (C) ec.rotation Vec3 components in wrong order (X/Y/Z) or wrong sign — a 35.0 tilt on the wrong axis points camera at sky not terrain; (D) EditorCamera on_enable() resets camera.position from camera.editor_position stale value if camera.editor_position was not synced correctly. PRECHECK REQUIRED (before writing any code): 1. Add a print statement logging ec.position, camera.position, camera.rotation at the end of _setup_ursina_camera_for_castle to confirm actual values. 2. Compare with the model viewer (tools/model_viewer_kenney.py lines ~720-735 which is known-working EditorCamera framing). 3. Add a temporary F2 overlay line showing castle grid_x/grid_y to confirm the building exists at camera setup time.
acceptance_test: python main.py --renderer ursina opens to castle clearly visible and centered.
F12 screenshot in agent log proves it.
qa_smoke --quick PASS.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Teal/green square 'grass rug' artefacts scattered across meadow
repro_steps: python main.py --renderer ursina (once camera is fixed per BUG-008)
Pan over any meadow area. Large flat teal/green-tinted squares visible on ground.
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Grass scatter reads as natural foliage — varied small tufts, flowers, no visible flat quad silhouette.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Solid-colored square quads (teal/green) scattered randomly. Reads as 'rug' or 'poster lying on ground', not as grass. Screenshot provided by Jaimie 2026-04-18.
pm_bug_tickets.pm_diagnosis_hints: Most likely cause: one or more GLB files promoted by Agent 15 are PLANAR QUADS (a flat mesh used as a 'sprite' in Kenney's Nature Kit) rather than 3D foliage geometry. When Ursina loads a planar quad with a texture and the 0.75 tint is applied as entity.color = rgb(0.75, 0.75, 0.75), the tinted texture can read as a flat colored rectangle at scatter scale. Candidate culprits (check these first): grass_tuft_a.glb, grass_tuft_b.glb, grass_flower_red.glb, grass_flower_yellow.glb — if these are texture-atlas quads rather than 3D geometry, they will show the alpha-silhouette problem. VISUAL ACCEPTANCE LOOP IS MANDATORY this time: 1. Launch python tools/model_viewer_kenney.py and find each grass/doodad model in the environment section. 2. Orbit the camera 90 degrees — if the model disappears edge-on, it is a flat quad. 3. Flat quads need billboard=True to look correct in scatter (or should be replaced with 3D geometry). OBJ-format models (rock_gy_cluster.obj etc.) are more likely to be true 3D and should be fine. 4. Screenshot every model from two angles (front + 90deg side) and log the results. 5. For any model that is a flat quad: either (a) set billboard=True in the scatter Entity spawned in ursina_renderer.py, or (b) replace with a 3D equivalent. Coordinate with Agent 03 if billboard flag needs adding to the scatter path.
acceptance_test: No flat square / 'rug' artefacts visible in meadow.
F12 screenshot from same camera angle as Jaimie's bug screenshot showing clean grass.
Ask verbatim: 'Does this look like real grass and nature?' Answer must be YES.
qa_smoke --quick PASS.

Building.construction_progress (float 0..1) ALREADY EXISTS at game/entities/buildings/base.py:172-187 (HP-curve derived). You consume it in ursina_renderer.py.

Resolver rule (required — must match what Agent 15 authors):
  progress == 0.0            -> plot_{w}x{h}_v1.json (where (w,h) = footprint_tiles)
  0.0 <  progress < 0.50     -> <building>_build_20_v1.json

  progress >= 1.0            -> <building>_v1.json (final, current behavior)

FALLBACK CHAIN (critical — missing prefab must NEVER crash):
  final_or_closer → 50% → 20% → size-matched plot → final (last resort).
Buildings other than peasant_house_small, castle, warrior_guild will not have intermediates this sprint — they must degrade to final silently.

PM-pre-reasoned architecture options for the swap:
  OPT A — Destroy+recreate on threshold cross. Pros: simplest; reuses existing load path. Cons: GC churn on crossing; momentary flicker possible. Risk: LOW. Effort: S.
  OPT B — Pre-spawn all 4 stages per building, toggle enabled=True/False. Pros: smooth. Cons: ~4x building entities at all times; memory; needs bookkeeping. Risk: MED (perf hit at kingdom scale). Effort: M.
  OPT C — Swap sub-meshes on one persistent root. Pros: cleanest; efficient. Cons: requires refactor of the prefab multi-piece loader to support hot-swap. Risk: HIGH. Effort: L.

  • Verify no z-fight between scatter and base green plane (y=-0.05 at rgb(0.2,0.5,0.2)).

=== WK32-BUG-007 — WATER TILE RENDERING ===
Current code (ursina_renderer.py _build_3d_terrain TileType.WATER branch) does:
  Entity(model=grass_models[0], color=water_tint, ...)
That draws a cross-plane grass mesh tinted blue. Fix: replace with a flat quad.

PM-pre-reasoned options for water:
  OPT A — One-tile quad per water tile, rotated flat (like fog), blue color, y≈0.005 so it sits just above the green base plane. Pros: trivial. Cons: many entities if many water tiles — but Agent 10 hasn't flagged it yet. Risk: LOW. Effort: S.
  OPT B — Single large water Entity spanning the union of water tiles (mesh built once). Pros: batched. Cons: requires extra mesh-building logic. Risk: MED. Effort: M.
  OPT C — Keep grass-as-water + darker tint + smaller scale so it reads as ripples. Not recommended — still wrong silhouette.
  PM recommends OPT A.

- env flag KINGDOM_URSINA_PREFAB_TEST=0 forces legacy render. Construction-phase path should respect that flag (keep it off, skip stage swap, fall through to legacy mesh/billboard).

round_meta.pm_note: Jaimie cannot preview the game after r2 because Ursina startup takes too long; terminal shows Panda3D OBJ cache misses/generation for new environment models. PM files hotfix; Agent 01 does not edit game code.

pm_status_summary.overall: Preview is blocked. Terminal output shows new OBJ environment models being read and converted to .bam cache files at startup; these Panda3D loader(error) lines are expected first-run cache misses, not necessarily fatal crashes. The actual blocker is that startup/render budget became too heavy after r2 dense grass + new OBJ assets.
diagnosis: The lines ':loader(error): Unable to open models_compressed/... .bam' are Panda3D checking for a compressed cache before creating it. They look scary but are usually cache-miss noise.
Agent 03 r2 changed grass to 3 clumps per eligible tile and explicitly stopped using URSINA_TERRAIN_SCATTER_STRIDE for grass. This likely multiplies grass Entity/model instantiation cost.

Agent 15 introduced several OBJ environment models. OBJ-to-BAM conversion on first run adds startup cost; if the game is killed before startup completes, later runs may repeatedly pay part of that cost.
Current config only exposes KINGDOM_URSINA_TERRAIN_SCATTER_STRIDE, but grass no longer respects it, so Jaimie has no practical low-density preview knob.
immediate_human_guidance: Do not treat the .bam cache-miss lines alone as fatal errors.
For any manual retest before the hotfix lands, use python main.py --renderer ursina --no-llm and allow the first run longer than normal so Panda can finish cache generation.
If still too slow to reach the window, wait for Agent 03's density/startup hotfix before further visual playtest.

pm_diagnosis_hints: Renderer code currently gates vertical terrain props on Visibility.VISIBLE only in _sync_visibility_gated_terrain; this explains trees disappearing in explored-but-not-currently-visible fog.

Chopping allowed only on tiles with Visibility != UNSEEN (SEEN or VISIBLE). Tree yields by growth: >=1.0 -> 10, >=0.75 -> 7, >=0.50 -> 5; cannot chop below 0.50. pm_bug_tickets.title: If builder peasant dies mid-construction, plot never completes (no replacement builder)
repro_steps: Run: python main.py --renderer ursina --no-llm
Wait for a House plot to start construction (partially built).
Allow the BuilderPeasant to die (enemy attack) before completion.

pm_bug_tickets.actual: Partially built plot never completes once the builder dies.

## Your Assignment

Build tools/perf_stress_test.py â€” a stress test tool that measures Ursina renderer performance at high unit counts.

## What To Build

A script that:
1. Launches the game with Ursina renderer in a non-interactive mode
2. Overrides enemy/hero counts to spawn N units (configurable: 200, 500, 1000)
3. Runs for 300 frames measuring frame time
4. Reports: average frame time (ms), min/max, 1% low FPS
5. Saves results to tools/perf_stress_baseline_wk47.json (under tools/ only)

## Implementation Approach

Use the existing headless sim + Ursina renderer. Key steps:
- Import SimEngine from game.sim_engine and manually add entities to create high unit counts (imports only; do not edit game package files)
- Import UrsinaRenderer and call update(snapshot) in a timed loop
- Before the loop, programmatically spawn extra enemies/heroes by appending to the engine live lists
- Time each update() call with time.perf_counter()
- Print a table of results and write JSON

Note: you may need to initialize pygame display (pygame.display.set_mode((1,1))) and Ursina (from ursina import Ursina; app = Ursina(...)) before using the renderer. Use web/docs or existing tools patterns under tools/ if you need bootstrap hints â€” do not edit game package files.

## CLI Interface

python tools/perf_stress_test.py --units 500 --frames 300

pm_agent_prompts.09:
You are Agent 09 (ArtDirector_Pixel_Animation_VFX), activated for sprint **wk47_unit_instancing_core**, round **wk47_r1_baseline_atlas**.

## Your Assignment

Build `game/graphics/unit_atlas.py` â€” a module that packs ALL unit sprite frames (heroes, enemies, workers) into a single 2048x2048 RGBA texture atlas at game boot, with a lookup dict mapping `(unit_type, class_key, action, frame_index)` to normalized UV coordinates.

## Why This Matters

The current renderer creates a separate Ursina Entity per unit with per-entity texture changes. We are moving to hardware instancing where ALL units share ONE texture (the atlas). Your atlas is the foundation the instanced shader (Agent 03, Wave 2) will sample from.

## What To Build

A class `UnitAtlasBuilder` with:
- Singleton pattern (built once at first access via `UnitAtlasBuilder.get()`)
- `atlas_surface` property returning `pygame.Surface` (2048x2048 RGBA)
- `lookup_uv(unit_type, class_key, action, frame_idx)` returning `(u_start, v_start, u_width, v_height)` in [0,1] normalized coordinates
- `frame_count` property returning total frames packed (for debug)

## Sprites To Pack

**Heroes** (5 classes x 5 actions x variable frames):
- Classes: `warrior`, `ranger`, `rogue`, `wizard`, `cleric`
- Actions: `idle`, `walk`, `attack`, `hurt`, `inside`
- Use: `HeroSpriteLibrary.clips_for(class_name, size=32)` returns dict of AnimationClip, each clip has `.frames` list of pygame.Surface

**Enemies** (6 types x variable actions x variable frames):
- Types: `goblin`, `wolf`, `skeleton`, `skeleton_archer`, `spider`, `bandit`
- Use: `EnemySpriteLibrary.clips_for(enemy_type, size=32)` returns dict of AnimationClip

**Workers** (3 types x variable actions x variable frames):
- Types: `peasant`, `guard`, `tax_collector`
- Use: `WorkerSpriteLibrary.clips_for(worker_type, size=32)` returns dict of AnimationClip

## Packing Algorithm

Simple row-based shelf packing:
- All sprites are 32x32 pixels
- 2048 / 32 = 64 frames per row
- 64 rows = 4096 total frame capacity (far more than needed)
- Fill left-to-right, top-to-bottom
- Track cursor_x, cursor_y

## EXACT Code To Write

File: `game/graphics/unit_atlas.py`

```python
"""Unit sprite atlas generator for instanced rendering (wk47)."""
from __future__ import annotations

import pygame
from typing import Dict, Tuple

from game.graphics.hero_sprites import HeroSpriteLibrary
from game.graphics.enemy_sprites import EnemySpriteLibrary
from game.graphics.worker_sprites import WorkerSpriteLibrary

# Normalized UV region on the atlas: (u_start, v_start, u_width, v_height)
UVRegion = Tuple[float, float, float, float]

# Key for looking up a specific frame: (unit_type, class_key, action, frame_index)
# Examples: ("hero", "warrior", "idle", 0), ("enemy", "goblin", "walk", 3)
AtlasKey = Tuple[str, str, str, int]

ATLAS_SIZE = 2048
FRAME_SIZE = 32


class UnitAtlasBuilder:
    """Packs all unit sprite frames into one GPU-friendly atlas."""

    _instance: "UnitAtlasBuilder | None" = None

    def __init__(self):
        self._atlas_surface: pygame.Surface | None = None
        self._uv_map: Dict[AtlasKey, UVRegion] = {}

    @classmethod
    def get(cls) -> "UnitAtlasBuilder":
        """Singleton access. Builds atlas on first call."""
        if cls._instance is None:
            cls._instance = cls()
            cls._instance._build()
        return cls._instance

    @property
    def atlas_surface(self) -> pygame.Surface:
        assert self._atlas_surface is not None
        return self._atlas_surface

    @property
    def frame_count(self) -> int:
        return len(self._uv_map)

    def lookup_uv(self, unit_type: str, class_key: str, action: str, frame_idx: int) -> UVRegion:
        """Look up the atlas UV region for a given unit frame."""
        key: AtlasKey = (unit_type, class_key, action, frame_idx)
        fallback: UVRegion = (0.0, 0.0, FRAME_SIZE / ATLAS_SIZE, FRAME_SIZE / ATLAS_SIZE)
        return self._uv_map.get(key, fallback)

    def _pack_frame(self, surf: pygame.Surface, key: AtlasKey, cursor: list[int]) -> None:
        """Blit one frame onto the atlas and record its UV region."""
        cx, cy = cursor
        if cx + FRAME_SIZE > ATLAS_SIZE:
            cx = 0
            cy += FRAME_SIZE
        self._atlas_surface.blit(surf, (cx, cy))
        self._uv_map[key] = (
            cx / ATLAS_SIZE,
            cy / ATLAS_SIZE,
            FRAME_SIZE / ATLAS_SIZE,
            FRAME_SIZE / ATLAS_SIZE,
        )
        cursor[0] = cx + FRAME_SIZE
        cursor[1] = cy

    def _build(self) -> None:
        self._atlas_surface = pygame.Surface((ATLAS_SIZE, ATLAS_SIZE), pygame.SRCALPHA)
        self._atlas_surface.fill((0, 0, 0, 0))
        cursor = [0, 0]  # [x, y] mutable for _pack_frame

        # Heroes
        for hc in ("warrior", "ranger", "rogue", "wizard", "cleric"):
            clips = HeroSpriteLibrary.clips_for(hc, size=FRAME_SIZE)
            for action, clip in clips.items():
                for fi, surf in enumerate(clip.frames):
                    self._pack_frame(surf, ("hero", hc, action, fi), cursor)

        # Enemies
        for et in ("goblin", "wolf", "skeleton", "skeleton_archer", "spider", "bandit"):
            clips = EnemySpriteLibrary.clips_for(et, size=FRAME_SIZE)
            for action, clip in clips.items():
                for fi, surf in enumerate(clip.frames):
                    self._pack_frame(surf, ("enemy", et, action, fi), cursor)

        # Workers
        for wt in ("peasant", "guard", "tax_collector"):
            clips = WorkerSpriteLibrary.clips_for(wt, size=FRAME_SIZE)
            for action, clip in clips.items():
                for fi, surf in enumerate(clip.frames):
                    self._pack_frame(surf, ("worker", wt, action, fi), cursor)
```

## CRITICAL Notes
- The atlas surface must use `pygame.SRCALPHA` flag (RGBA)
- All frames are 32x32 â€” do not resize
- UV coordinates are NORMALIZED to [0,1] range (divide pixel coords by ATLAS_SIZE)
- The UV origin (0,0) is TOP-LEFT of the atlas (pygame convention matches)

## Files You May Edit
- `game/graphics/unit_atlas.py` (NEW)

## Files You Must NOT Edit
- `game/graphics/hero_sprites.py`, `enemy_sprites.py`, `worker_sprites.py` (read only)
- `game/graphics/ursina_renderer.py` (Agent 03 wires this)

   ```powershell
   python -c "import pygame; pygame.init(); pygame.display.set_mode((1,1)); from game.graphics.unit_atlas import UnitAtlasBuilder; a = UnitAtlasBuilder.get(); print(f'Atlas: {a.atlas_surface.get_size()}, Frames: {a.frame_count}'); uv = a.lookup_uv('hero', 'warrior', 'idle', 0); print(f'Warrior idle 0 UV: {uv}'); assert uv[2] > 0; print('PASS')"
   ```

pm_bug_tickets.title: Accepted direct prompt commands do not reliably produce committed hero behavior
repro_steps: Run python main.py --provider mock.
Open hero chat.
Ask go home / buy potions / explore east.
Hero response says accepted or command will be followed, but hero does not move to home/market/east and may immediately fight/rest elsewhere.
pm_bug_tickets.expected: If UI says command accepted/will be followed, the hero should receive a committed deterministic action target for a short window, or the UI must say the command was refused/redirected.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Hero speech and UI feedback often imply acceptance, but normal AI behavior overrides or no actionable target is set.
acceptance_test: Accepted go home sets a committed movement/retreat target to known safety/home.
Accepted buy potions moves to known market or buys if already there; if impossible, response/feedback clearly refuses with reason.
Accepted explore east sets an eastward committed destination and is not immediately overwritten by routine AI unless urgent safety/combat rules explicitly redirect.
UI feedback never says accepted if no physical action was actually committed.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Direct prompt target/refusal language is inconsistent with known places
repro_steps: Run python main.py --provider mock.
Ask buy potions when hero has gold and knows market.
Hero may claim market is too far or not safe instead of attempting movement or giving a precise refusal.
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Direct prompt validator/prompt/mock provider should align response with known-place context and validated executable target.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Responses sometimes cite incorrect/weak reasons despite known places and feasible safe commands.
acceptance_test: Mock provider direct prompt responses for go home, buy potions, and explore east include executable/validated intent when context supports it.
If context does not support execution, response uses a clear refusal/redirect reason matching validator output.

pm_status_summary.summary: WK51 R1 — Parallel design guardrails: Agent 02 acceptance checklist (docs only); Agent 04 determinism baseline review (log only).

pm_agent_prompts.02:
You are activated for sprint `wk51_attachment_ux_phase3`, round `wk51_r1_design_guardrails`. Your task is **acceptance criteria only — no code edits**.

Read [`.cursor/plans/wk51_phase_3_attachment_94c67db4.plan.md`](.cursor/plans/wk51_phase_3_attachment_94c67db4.plan.md) (full plan), and [`.cursor/plans/wk49_hero_profile_roadmap_6f3a1b2c.plan.md`](.cursor/plans/wk49_hero_profile_roadmap_6f3a1b2c.plan.md) Phase 3 section, then write a **player-facing 5–7 point acceptance checklist** under `docs/sprint/wk51_attachment_ux_acceptance.md`. The checklist must be verifiable in **5 minutes of manual play with `python main.py --provider mock`**.

Required acceptance points (you may rephrase but must cover all of these):

1. With a hero selected, a Pin button is visible in the top-right of the left hero panel and reads as either pinned or not pinned at a glance.
2. Clicking Pin while a hero is selected does not change anything in the world (no AI/movement/economy effect).
3. After pinning a hero and clicking somewhere else (closing the panel), a Recall button appears on the bottom HUD with that hero's name.
4. Clicking Recall reopens that hero's left panel AND moves the camera to center on them in less than one second, in both 3D (Ursina) and 2D (Pygame) renderers.
5. If a different hero is pinned afterwards, the Recall button updates to the new hero (single slot replaces).
6. If the pinned hero dies, Recall shows their name with "(fallen)" greyed out for about 10 seconds, then disappears.
7. Pinning never makes the AI prefer a hero, never changes combat outcomes, never alters bounty selection. Two seeded mock-provider runs with and without pinning must produce identical hero behavior.

You may only create or edit files under the documentation tree and your own agent log. Do not modify application source, tools, tests, assets, or project entry/config modules.

**Self-verify** (mandatory before claiming done):

```powershell
Test-Path docs/sprint/wk51_attachment_ux_acceptance.md
python -m json.tool .cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_02_GameDirector_ProductOwner.json
```

Update your log at `sprints["wk51_attachment_ux_phase3"].rounds["wk51_r1_design_guardrails"]` with required orchestrator fields (`sprint_id`, `round_id`, `status`, `what_i_changed`, `commands_run`, `evidence`, `blockers`, `follow_ups`). Then run your SDK completion receipt.
pm_agent_prompts.04:
You are activated for sprint `wk51_attachment_ux_phase3`, round `wk51_r1_design_guardrails`. Your task is a **determinism review** of the planned changes — log only, no code edits.

The plan introduces a **UI-only** `PinSlot` (in the PinSlot module (plan Data Contract; presentation-only)) and three engine action tokens (`pin_hero`, `unpin_hero`, `recall_pinned_hero`). Pin state must NOT influence simulation. The recall action mutates `selected_hero` (already non-deterministic player input) and centers the camera (presentation-only).

Confirm in your log entry:

1. **No new wall-clock time.** PinSlot.update_liveness and pinned_at_ms must use sim milliseconds from the timebase (never wall-clock or pygame tick APIs). Verify by reading the planned snippet in the parent plan.
2. **No new RNG.** `PinSlot` uses no random sources.
3. **Sim boundary stays clean.** PinSlot belongs in the UI/presentation package, outside the determinism boundary (entities, systems, AI, and authoritative sim).
4. **Action handler is read-only on sim state.** `recall_pinned_hero` reads `self.heroes` to find the hero by `hero_id`, sets `self.selected_hero`, and sets camera state. Setting `selected_hero` is already a player-input mutation outside sim invariants.
5. **MP-readiness note**: in a future multiplayer setting, pin slot is per-client UI state. It must not be serialized into the lockstep state stream.

Run:

```powershell
python tools/determinism_guard.py
```

…and confirm it currently PASSES (baseline). Then state in your log: "Approved with no required code changes; the proposed `PinSlot` design is determinism-clean as long as it stays in the UI layer and uses sim-time milliseconds exclusively."

You may NOT edit any code. Update your log with required orchestrator fields, validate JSON with `python -m json.tool`, then run your SDK completion receipt.

pm_agent_prompts.03:
You are activated for sprint `wk51_attachment_ux_phase3`, round `wk51_r2_data_engine_plumbing`. You implement the data contract and engine plumbing. **You do NOT touch UI rendering** — Agent 08 owns that in Wave 2.

### What you build

#### 3.1 New file: PinSlot module (see plan Data Contract)

Use exactly the contents from the parent plan's "Data Contract" section. Important details:

- Place it under the UI package (NOT under `game/sim/`). This file is presentation/UI state, NOT simulation.
- Time argument is always **passed in** (caller provides `now_ms`); the module itself does not import `sim_now_ms` to keep it pure-data and trivially unit-testable.
- Liveness check: `PinSlot.update_liveness(hero_alive: bool, now_ms: int)`. The caller (HUD) determines `hero_alive` by checking whether the pinned `hero_id` still exists in hero_profiles_by_id from game_state.

#### 3.2 New tests: [`tests/test_wk51_pin_slot.py`](tests/test_wk51_pin_slot.py)

Cover at minimum:

- `test_pin_then_unpin_clears_id`: pin("h1", now_ms=1000), assert hero_id=="h1", unpin(), assert hero_id is None.
- `test_pin_replaces_previous`: pin("h1", 1000), pin("h2", 2000), assert hero_id=="h2" and pinned_at_ms==2000 and fallen_since_ms is None.
- `test_update_liveness_marks_fallen`: pin("h1", 1000), update_liveness(hero_alive=False, now_ms=2000), assert is_fallen() and fallen_since_ms==2000 and hero_id=="h1" (still set).
- `test_update_liveness_auto_clears_after_window`: pin, mark fallen, then update_liveness(hero_alive=False, now_ms=fallen_since_ms+10_000); assert hero_id is None.
- `test_update_liveness_within_window_keeps_pin`: pin, mark fallen, update_liveness(False, now_ms=fallen_since_ms+9_999); assert hero_id is still set.
- `test_update_liveness_resurrected_hero_does_not_reset_fallen`: do NOT clear `fallen_since_ms` if `hero_alive` flips back to True after death (heroes do not resurrect in this game). The slot just times out and clears. (Document this as the chosen behavior.)
- `test_pin_slot_imports_no_pygame_no_ursina`: importing PinSlot must not import `pygame` or `ursina`. Implement by checking `sys.modules` after a fresh import.

#### 3.3 Engine action routing — edit [`game/engine.py`](game/engine.py)

Look for the existing pattern that handles HUD action strings (e.g. `close_selection`, `quit`). Search for `def handle_click` or where actions like `close_selection` are consumed. You will add three new tokens: `pin_hero`, `unpin_hero`, `recall_pinned_hero`.

The HUD owns the `PinSlot`. Engine merely routes the click and reads the slot when handling `recall_pinned_hero`. Suggested additions (sketch — match the surrounding code style):

```python
# In GameEngine, near where other HUD actions are dispatched:
def _handle_pin_action(self, action: str) -> None:
    sel = getattr(self, "selected_hero", None)
    pin_slot = self.hud._pin_slot
    now_ms = int(sim_now_ms())
    if action == "pin_hero":
        if sel is None:
            return
        hid = str(getattr(sel, "hero_id", "") or "").strip()
        if not hid:
            return
        pin_slot.pin(hid, now_ms)
    elif action == "unpin_hero":
        pin_slot.unpin()
    elif action == "recall_pinned_hero":
        if pin_slot.is_fallen() or pin_slot.hero_id is None:
            return
        target = self._find_hero_by_id(pin_slot.hero_id)
        if target is None:
            return
        self.selected_hero = target
        self.center_camera_on_world_pos(float(target.x), float(target.y))
```

And add the lookup helper:

```python
def _find_hero_by_id(self, hero_id: str) -> object | None:
    for h in self.heroes:
        if str(getattr(h, "hero_id", "")) == str(hero_id):
            return h
    return None
```

#### 3.4 Camera centering helper

Add one method to `GameEngine`:

```python
def center_camera_on_world_pos(self, world_x: float, world_y: float) -> None:
    """Snap camera to world (x, y). Both renderers must implement (Pygame: directly;
    Ursina: forwarded via UrsinaApp)."""
    # Pygame path: existing GameEngine camera state already tracks a center/offset.
    # Inspect existing update_camera / camera_x / camera_y fields in this file and set them.
    ...
```

For the **Pygame** path: locate the existing camera fields (likely `self.camera_x`, `self.camera_y` or a `Camera` object). Set them so the world point is centered in the viewport. Account for current zoom.

For the **Ursina** path: edit [`game/graphics/ursina_app.py`](game/graphics/ursina_app.py) to add an analogous `center_camera_on_world_pos(world_x, world_y)` that updates the Ursina camera's `position` (or whatever the existing camera-pan state is — search for current pan / WASD camera handling and re-use it). Then in `GameEngine.center_camera_on_world_pos`, dispatch to the right path based on the active renderer (the existing engine already knows which renderer is active because it owns `ursina_app` reference or analogous).

If the dispatching shape is awkward, add a thin protocol method on `GameCommands` (the `EngineBackedGameCommands` shim already exists per `.cursor/rules/02-project-layout.mdc`) and call through that. Do whatever is cleanest given the existing structure — but the **end-to-end behavior** must be: `engine.center_camera_on_world_pos(x, y)` results in both renderers' next frame drawing with that point at screen center.

#### 3.5 New tests: [`tests/test_wk51_recall_routing.py`](tests/test_wk51_recall_routing.py)

- `test_pin_hero_action_records_selected_id`: build a fake engine with two heroes (with hero_ids), set `selected_hero = heroes[0]`, dispatch `pin_hero`, assert `engine.hud._pin_slot.hero_id == heroes[0].hero_id`.
- `test_recall_finds_hero_and_sets_selected`: pin heroes[0], select heroes[1], dispatch `recall_pinned_hero`, assert `engine.selected_hero is heroes[0]`.
- `test_recall_calls_center_camera`: monkey-patch `engine.center_camera_on_world_pos` to record calls, recall, assert called with (heroes[0].x, heroes[0].y).
- `test_recall_does_nothing_when_pin_empty`: empty slot, dispatch recall, assert `selected_hero` unchanged.
- `test_recall_does_nothing_when_fallen`: pin "ghost_id" (not in heroes), call `pin_slot.update_liveness(hero_alive=False, now_ms=...)`, dispatch recall, assert no selection change.
- `test_pin_unpin_pin_replaces`: pin h0, pin h1, assert slot.hero_id == h1.hero_id.

These tests can use lightweight fakes — no need to spin a full `GameEngine`. Build a minimal mock with `.heroes`, `.selected_hero`, `.hud._pin_slot`, and the new helper methods extracted into a free function or a tiny dispatcher class if that's cleaner.

### Determinism guardrails for you

- **Do not** import `time` or `pygame.time` in `pin_slot.py` or in any new test file's helper code that simulates sim_now_ms — pass int milliseconds explicitly.
- The new engine method `_handle_pin_action` lives in `GameEngine` (presentation layer), so wall-clock IS allowed there in principle, but **prefer `sim_now_ms()`** to keep behavior testable from headless harnesses.

### Verify

```powershell
python -m pytest tests/test_wk51_pin_slot.py tests/test_wk51_recall_routing.py -v
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit 1 }
python tools/determinism_guard.py
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit 1 }
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit 1 }
```

All three must PASS. Also confirm no new pygame/ursina imports were added to the PinSlot module path from the sprint plan:

```powershell
Select-String -Path game/ui/pin_slot.py -Pattern "pygame|ursina"
```

…must produce no output.

Update your log at `sprints["wk51_attachment_ux_phase3"].rounds["wk51_r2_data_engine_plumbing"]` with required orchestrator fields, validate JSON, then run your SDK completion receipt.

pm_agent_prompts.08:
You are activated for sprint `wk51_attachment_ux_phase3`, round `wk51_r3_ui_pin_recall`. **Depends on Agent 03's Wave 1 already being merged.** You implement the visible UI: the pin button on the HeroPanel header and the recall button on the bottom HUD, plus the fallen-state countdown rendering.

### Files you may edit

- [`game/ui/hud.py`](game/ui/hud.py)
- [`game/ui/hero_panel.py`](game/ui/hero_panel.py)
- New tests under `tests/` (UI-level)

### What you build

#### 8.1 Pin button on the HeroPanel header

Add a small toggle button to the **top-right** of the left panel header. Design constraints:

- Size: 20×20 px.
- Position: `(left_rect.right - size - 12, left_rect.y + 6, size, size)`. **The close X is already at `left_rect.right - size - 6`**, so the pin button must sit immediately to its left, NOT overlap. After your change, the order from left to right along the top edge should be: `[panel content title]` ... `[Pin] [X]`.
- Visual:
  - **Pinned** state: filled gold circle (color: `COLOR_GOLD = (220, 180, 50)`), border in `_frame_outer`, with white "P" glyph centered (small font).
  - **Unpinned** state: hollow circle with `_frame_inner` border, dim grey "P" glyph (color `(150, 150, 160)`).
- Click region: must be hit-testable in `HUD.handle_click`. Store the rect in `self.pin_button_rect` after rendering, just like `left_close_rect` is stored.

Implementation pattern: follow `_render_left_close_button` exactly. Add a sibling method `_render_pin_button(self, surface, left_rect, game_state)` in `HUD` that:

1. Reads `self._pin_slot.hero_id`.
2. Reads currently selected `hero_id`.
3. If `pin_slot.hero_id == selected.hero_id`, draw "pinned" style; else draw "unpinned" style.
4. Stores `self.pin_button_rect = pygame.Rect(...)`.

In `HUD.render`, call `self._render_pin_button(surface, left, game_state)` only when a hero is selected (right after `_render_left_close_button(surface, left)`).

In `HUD.handle_click`, before falling through to the existing `left_close_rect` check, add:

```python
if getattr(self, "pin_button_rect", None) is not None and self.pin_button_rect.collidepoint((x, y)):
    sel = game_state.get("selected_hero")
    sel_id = str(getattr(sel, "hero_id", "") or "")
    if not sel_id:
        return None
    if self._pin_slot.hero_id == sel_id:
        return "unpin_hero"
    return "pin_hero"
```

Important: the click test for `pin_button_rect` must come **before** the `left_close_rect` test because they are adjacent and a misclick must not bubble to the wrong action.

#### 8.2 Recall button on the bottom HUD

Resize the bottom-bar layout so the recall button fits between the minimap and the command bar.

In `HUD._layout_rects_for_screen`, change the bottom-row geometry:

```python
# Current:
# minimap_size = max(64, bottom_h - 2 * margin)
# minimap = pygame.Rect(margin, bottom.y + margin, minimap_size, minimap_size)
# cmd_x = minimap.right + gutter
# cmd_w = max(0, speed_rect.left - cmd_x - gutter)
# command = pygame.Rect(cmd_x, bottom.y + margin, cmd_w, minimap_size)

# After:
RECALL_BTN_W = 180
RECALL_BTN_H = minimap_size  # match minimap height for vertical alignment
recall = pygame.Rect(minimap.right + gutter, minimap.y, RECALL_BTN_W, RECALL_BTN_H)
cmd_x = recall.right + gutter
cmd_w = max(0, speed_rect.left - cmd_x - gutter)
command = pygame.Rect(cmd_x, bottom.y + margin, cmd_w, minimap_size)
return top, bottom, left, right, minimap, command, speed_rect, recall
```

Update `_layout_rects_for_screen` to return 8 rects (was 7). All callers must be updated:

- `_compute_layout` (just unpack and return).
- `virtual_pointer_in_hud_chrome` — add `recall` to `regions` so Ursina pointer routing knows to mask it.
- `render` — call `self._render_recall_button(surface, recall, game_state)` after the minimap is drawn, only when `self._pin_slot.hero_id is not None`.
- `handle_click` — hit-test the recall rect early and return `"recall_pinned_hero"` when clicked AND the slot is not fallen. When fallen, click does nothing (visual is already disabled).

The recall button's rendered content:

- **Active (pinned, alive)**: 9-slice button using existing `_button_tex_normal` / `_button_tex_hover` / `_button_tex_pressed`. Label: `"↩ {hero_name}"` where the arrow is a small gold glyph (use `theme.font_small`). Truncate the name with `truncate_panel_line(..., max_chars=14)`.
- **Fallen**: 9-slice button with `_button_tex_pressed` style and 60% alpha overlay. Label: `"{hero_name} (fallen)"`, color `(160, 160, 165)`.

Look up the hero's display name like this (do NOT use raw `Hero.name`, use the profile snapshot):

```python
profiles = game_state.get("hero_profiles_by_id") or {}
prof = profiles.get(self._pin_slot.hero_id)
name = "Hero"
if prof is not None:
    idn = getattr(prof, "identity", None)
    if idn is not None:
        name = str(getattr(idn, "name", "Hero"))
```

Liveness update happens once per render frame, **before** drawing the button:

```python
hero_alive = self._pin_slot.hero_id in (game_state.get("hero_profiles_by_id") or {})
self._pin_slot.update_liveness(hero_alive=hero_alive, now_ms=int(sim_now_ms()))
```

If `self._pin_slot.hero_id is None` after that update, do not render the recall button at all.

#### 8.3 Tooltip on hover (optional, only if cheap)

If your existing tooltip system already supports a one-line hover tooltip, add tooltips: pin → `"Pin hero (one-tap return)"`; recall → `"Return to {name}"`. If no tooltip system exists, skip it — do not invent one.

#### 8.4 New UI tests: [`tests/test_wk51_ui_pin_recall.py`](tests/test_wk51_ui_pin_recall.py)

You'll need a headless Pygame surface (`pygame.Surface((1920, 1080))`) and a fake `game_state`. Tests:

- `test_pin_button_click_returns_pin_action`: select a hero with `hero_id="h0"`, render HUD, click the stored `pin_button_rect`, assert HUD.handle_click returns `"pin_hero"`.
- `test_unpin_when_already_pinned`: pre-pin h0 in `_pin_slot`, render with selected=h0, click pin button, assert returns `"unpin_hero"`.
- `test_recall_button_invisible_when_no_pin`: `_pin_slot.hero_id is None`, render, click where the recall button would be, assert HUD.handle_click does NOT return `"recall_pinned_hero"`.
- `test_recall_button_returns_action_when_visible`: pin h0, render, click recall rect, assert returns `"recall_pinned_hero"`.
- `test_recall_button_disabled_when_fallen`: pin h0 then mark fallen via `update_liveness(False, now_ms=...)`, render, click recall, assert HUD.handle_click does NOT return `"recall_pinned_hero"` (returns None).
- `test_layout_recall_does_not_overlap_command`: compute layout for 1920x1080, assert `recall.right < command.x` and `command.width > 200`.

Use small mocks for `selected_hero` / profiles. You do NOT need a full `GameEngine`.

### Verify (mandatory before claiming done)

```powershell
python -m pytest tests/test_wk51_ui_pin_recall.py -v
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit 1 }
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick
if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit 1 }
```

Also capture screenshots (this is **mandatory** evidence):

```powershell
python tools/capture_screenshots.py --scenario ui_panels --seed 3 --out docs/screenshots/wk51_attachment_ux --size 1920x1080 --ticks 480
```

Then visually open the resulting PNGs and confirm:

- `ui_panels_seed3.png` (or whichever the scenario produces) shows the bottom HUD layout with no overlapping rects (recall area should be empty when no pin exists in the captured scenario).
- If the screenshot scenario does not naturally pin a hero, that's fine — pin/recall visual evidence will come from Jaimie's manual smoke. You only need to confirm the bottom-bar layout did not regress.

If `ui_panels` does not currently show a hero selected, ALSO capture a manual screenshot via the screenshot tool with a scenario that selects a hero (check the screenshot scenario definitions (coordinate with Tools if adding presets) for a scenario that includes hero selection; if none exists, document this gap in your log under follow-ups but do not invent a new scenario).

Update your log at `sprints["wk51_attachment_ux_phase3"].rounds["wk51_r3_ui_pin_recall"]` with required orchestrator fields, including the exact screenshot file paths in `evidence`. Validate JSON, then run your SDK completion receipt.

pm_agent_prompts.10:
You are activated for sprint `wk51_attachment_ux_phase3`, round `wk51_r4_qa_perf` (LOW intelligence — consult only, no code edits).

The new UI work adds two HUD elements (pin button, recall button) and a per-frame `_pin_slot.update_liveness()` call. Verify there is no per-frame allocation regression.

Run:

```powershell
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick
python tools/perf_benchmark.py
```

Confirm:

1. Sim ms/tick is unchanged from baseline (within ±5%).
2. The `update_liveness` call is O(1) — it only checks `hero_id in dict` (which is O(1)) and a couple of integer comparisons. Confirm by reading PinSlot source.
3. The recall button text surface is **cached or rebuilt only on slot change**, not every frame. Read Agent 08's `_render_recall_button` and confirm; if you see `theme.font_small.render(...)` called every frame with the same text, file a follow-up bug ticket (do NOT fix yourself; that's Agent 08's domain).

### Targeted new test you author yourself: [the new WK51 determinism test module](tests/test_wk51_pin_determinism.py)

This test proves pin/recall has zero sim impact.

```python
"""WK51: prove pinning a hero does not change sim outcomes."""
import os
os.environ.setdefault("SDL_VIDEODRIVER", "dummy")
from game.sim_engine import SimEngine
# (or whatever the right way is to spin up two seeded headless sims; mirror tools/observe_sync.py)


def test_two_runs_match_with_and_without_pin():
    snap_baseline = run_seeded_sim(seed=3, ticks=480, pin_first_hero=False)
    snap_pinned = run_seeded_sim(seed=3, ticks=480, pin_first_hero=True)
    # Heroes' final positions, HP, gold, intent must all match exactly.
    assert _heroes_state(snap_baseline) == _heroes_state(snap_pinned)
```

The `run_seeded_sim` helper should:

1. Build a SimEngine with `seed=3`.
2. Optionally pin the first hero by setting `engine.hud._pin_slot.pin(heroes[0].hero_id, now_ms=0)` and triggering one `recall_pinned_hero` action mid-run.
3. Run N ticks.
4. Return a deterministic dict of `{hero_id: (round(x,2), round(y,2), hp, gold, intent_slug)}`.

If your existing test harness already has a "two seeded runs" pattern (search existing engine tests and existing hero profile integration tests), reuse it; do not invent new infrastructure.

**One targeted fix in your owned file `game/ui/hud.py`.**

## Fix: Pin button → 📌 emoji

Search for `def _render_pin_button` in `game/ui/hud.py` (around line 543). Currently it draws a `pygame.draw.circle` with a 'P' text glyph. Replace this with a 📌 emoji (U+1F4CC).

**Implementation steps:**
1. At the start of the method, resolve an emoji font (cache on `self` to avoid per-frame allocation):
```python
if not hasattr(self, '_pin_emoji_font') or getattr(self, '_pin_emoji_font_size', 0) != pin_size:
    try:
        self._pin_emoji_font = pygame.font.SysFont('segoeuiemoji,segoeui,noto color emoji,arial', pin_size)
    except Exception:
        self._pin_emoji_font = None
    self._pin_emoji_font_size = pin_size
```
2. Render the emoji:
```python
emoji_surf = None
if self._pin_emoji_font is not None:
    try:
        emoji_surf = self._pin_emoji_font.render('\U0001f4cc', True, (255, 255, 255))
    except Exception:
        emoji_surf = None
```
3. If `emoji_surf` is None or `emoji_surf.get_width() <= 4`, fall back to the existing circle+P code unchanged.
4. Otherwise blit the emoji centered in `self.pin_button_rect`:
   - **Pinned** (selected hero matches pin slot): blit at full alpha.
   - **Unpinned**: `s = emoji_surf.copy(); s.set_alpha(128); surface.blit(s, dest_pos)`.
5. Remove the `pygame.draw.circle` and `TextLabel.get_surface(..., 'P', ...)` calls from the non-fallback path.


pm_agent_prompts.08:
You are activated for sprint wk52_attachment_phase3_radar_alerts_memorial, round wk52_r3_layout_fixes (single-agent hotfix wave).

CONTEXT — three layout bugs visible in `.cursor/human_provided/wk52 screenshot 2.PNG` (READ THE PNG FIRST):

  Bug A: The pinned hero watch card renders BEHIND the minimap instead of above it. Geometrically the card_top y is correct (minimap.y - card_height), but visually the minimap chrome covers it.

  Bug B: When NO hero is pinned, the left 'Heroes' panel extends DOWN past the minimap top, overlapping the minimap. In game/ui/hud.py `_layout_rects_for_screen` the `left_h` shrink only happens inside `if self._pin_slot.hero_id is not None`. The shrink must apply unconditionally based on minimap.y.

  Bug C: The minimap and the hero card are offset from the screen edges by `margin` on the left and bottom. The user wants the minimap to TOUCH the left edge (x=0) and the bottom edge (y = h - minimap_size), and the hero card to TOUCH the left edge as well. No margin on those edges. Other gutters (between minimap and recall button, etc.) stay as they are.

ALSO READ: `.cursor/human_provided/wk52 minimap and hero map sizes.PNG` (the original mockup) to confirm the intended look.

READ-ONLY REFS:
  - game/ui/hud.py constants block (~lines 22-32: LEFT_COL_W, WATCH_MINIMAP_SIZE, WATCH_CARD_*) — do NOT change these sizes; they were set by user.
  - game/ui/hud.py `_layout_rects_for_screen` (~lines 229-296)
  - game/ui/hud.py `_render_watch_card_chrome` (~lines 661-770) — confirm cx = minimap_rect.x picks up the new x=0
  - game/ui/hud.py `render()` (~lines 1140-1160) — render-order call site for watch card, minimap panel, radar, recall
  - game/engine_facades/render_coordinator.py `_render_hero_minimap` (~line 142) — drawn after `e.hud.render()`; confirm it does not extend above its rect into the card area

DELIVERABLES:

  1. game/ui/hud.py `_layout_rects_for_screen`:
     - Change minimap to: `minimap = pygame.Rect(0, h - minimap_size, minimap_size, minimap_size)` (touches left + bottom; no margin on those two edges).
     - Restructure the left_h calculation so it ALWAYS shrinks to make room for the minimap, not only when pinned. New rule: `cap_y = minimap.y; if pinned: cap_y = min(cap_y, card_top); left_h = max(0, cap_y - top_h)`.
     - Recall/memorial/command rects already use `minimap.right + gutter` — they will auto-shift since minimap.x = 0 and minimap.right = minimap_size. Verify the gutter remains between minimap and recall (do NOT collapse it to 0).

  2. Fix Bug A (card behind minimap). Investigate render order in `HUD.render()`:
     - The watch card chrome (`_render_watch_card_chrome`) must be blitted BEFORE the minimap panel background draw if the card area overlaps any portion the panel fills. With minimap.y at h-224 and card occupying y=(minimap.y - card_h)..minimap.y, the card sits strictly above minimap.y; if the panel background still draws over the card area, fix it to clip to the minimap rect only.
     - Also confirm there is no `Panel` chrome around the minimap that draws a border/fill extending UP into the card region. If found, narrow it to minimap rect bounds.
     - If the bug is actually the render_coordinator `_render_hero_minimap` writing into a rect larger than `watch_card_map_rect`, clip it.

  3. tools/screenshot_scenarios.py: in `scenario_wk52_pin_alerts` (~line 1140), ADD a 5th Shot named `wk52_left_menu_unpinned.png` whose `apply` callback runs `_clear_dynamic_entities` style setup but does NOT pin a hero (skip `eng.hud._pin_slot.pin(...)` and `pinned_name`). This Shot proves Bug B is fixed — Heroes panel must end above minimap, no overlap. Include in returned list. Update the scenario_name dispatch in `get_scenario` if needed (it already handles 'wk52_pin_alerts').

GATES (all exit 0 required):
  - python -m pytest tests/test_wk52_watch_card.py tests/test_wk52_memorial_card.py tests/test_wk52_event_plumbing.py -v
  - python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick
  - python tools/capture_screenshots.py --scenario wk52_pin_alerts --seed 3 --out docs/screenshots/wk52_phase3 --size 1920x1080 --ticks 480

MANDATORY SCREENSHOT VERIFICATION (HARD REQUIREMENT — round status=blocked if skipped or any verdict is negative):
After the capture command, OPEN each PNG (use your read_file or image-view tool on the path) and write a one-line verdict per shot under the receipt's `evidence.screenshot_verdict` map. Required keys + acceptance per shot:

  - `wk52_watch_card_expanded.png`: 'PASS' iff hero card is fully visible ABOVE the minimap (no clipping, not behind), card touches left edge x=0, minimap touches left x=0 and bottom y=h-1.
  - `wk52_watch_card_minimized.png`: 'PASS' iff 18px header peeks above minimap and same edge contacts hold.

For each shot include both 'verdict' (PASS/FAIL) and 'note' (1 short sentence describing what you saw). Example:

pm_agent_prompts.08:
You are activated for sprint wk52_attachment_phase3_radar_alerts_memorial, round wk52_r4_remove_right_menu (single-agent, sequential).

READ FIRST (in order):
  - `.cursor/human_provided/wk52 screenshot 3.PNG` — the CURRENT state. Note the wide right panel with hero portrait + Get Commission button + Recall, and the cramped left hero card with stacked HP/XP/Lv/Mana lines.
  - `.cursor/human_provided/wk52 remove right menu and left menu updates.PNG` — the DESIRED state (annotated). Red diagonal cross-hatch on the right side = REMOVE that. Red rectangle around left column = restructured hero card + minimap + chat box at bottom. The mockup chat box is solid black because it is empty in a fresh session.
  - `game/ui/hud.py` constants and `_layout_rects_for_screen` (~lines 22-32, 229-300)
  - `game/ui/hud.py` `_render_watch_card_chrome` (~lines 661-770) — current vertical stat layout to be reflowed into a 2-column 2-row grid.
  - `game/ui/hud.py` `_render_radar_minimap` (~line 745) — entity dots; needs a terrain base pass first.
  - `game/ui/hud.py` `handle_click` and `_render_right_panel_overview` plus the right-panel render call site in `render()` (~line 1140).
  - `game/ui/chat_panel.py` — ChatPanel render entry; you will repoint it from the right rect to a new chat rect inside the left column.
  - `game/engine_facades/render_coordinator.py` — confirm right panel rendering path; remove or no-op when right_w=0.
  - `game/world.py` (read-only) for tile type access (TileType.GRASS / TREE / WATER / etc.) used by the new minimap terrain pass.

DELIVERABLES (all five required for status=done):

D1. HERO CARD CHEVRON DOES NOT CLOSE OTHER MENUS.
  Repro from screenshot 3: clicking the chevron to expand/minimize the watch card currently dismisses the right panel / clears selection / closes other open menus. The toggle must be PURELY visual on the watch card. In `handle_click`, when the click hits the watch card header chevron region, only flip `self._watch_card_expanded` and consume the event; do NOT touch `right_panel_visible`, `selected_hero`, `_micro_view`, build catalog, etc. Add a regression test (or a comment-block manual check) confirming a chevron click leaves all other HUD state unchanged.

D2. WATCH CARD MAP CLICK SELECTS THE HERO AT THAT WORLD POSITION.
  Today the live mini-view inside `watch_card_map_rect` is render-only. The user wants clicking a different hero passing the pinned hero in that rect to select that other hero (just like clicking on the main world map). Implementation:
    - The hero card map is rendered by `render_coordinator._render_hero_minimap` with the pinned hero centered, zoom=1.0. Persist the inverse transform: when `_render_hero_minimap` runs, store on HUD (or a new `WatchCardMapTransform` dataclass) the world-space rect that corresponds to the rendered `watch_card_map_rect` (center_x, center_y, world_w_visible, world_h_visible, plus the pixel rect).
    - In `HUD.handle_click`, if the click is inside `watch_card_map_rect`, project the click pixel back to world (wx, wy). Find the closest hero in the heroes list on game_state within ~24px radius (or ~half a tile). Return action ('select_hero_at_world', wx, wy) or similar. The engine handler in `game/engine.py handle_hud_action()` resolves the closest hero and sets `selected_hero`. Do NOT change pin slot — pinning is independent of selection.
    - Edge case: if the click hits inside the rect but no hero is found, do nothing (don't deselect).

D3. RADAR MINIMAP TERRAIN BASE PASS.
  In `_render_radar_minimap`, before drawing entity dots, render a generic terrain layer using a sampled grid over the world. Suggested approach: iterate over a downsampled grid of tiles (e.g., 1 tile per ~2 radar pixels), look up `world.tile_at(tx, ty)` and pick a color:
    - Grass / generic terrain: very dark green RGB (25, 50, 25)
    - Trees: dark green dot slightly lighter than terrain RGB (40, 75, 40), drawn as a 1-2px dot ON TOP of the grass base
    - Water: deep blue RGB (25, 50, 100)
    - Mountain placeholder (no current TileType): reserved color RGB (80, 80, 90); add a TODO comment with `# TODO: render mountain tiles when world.TileType.MOUNTAIN exists`
  Keep this fast: the existing minimap loop already iterates entities; don't loop over every world tile every frame. Cache a static terrain Surface (size = minimap rect) on HUD keyed on world dimensions, regenerate only when world changes (or just on first paint). On each frame, blit cached surface, then draw entity dots on top. This satisfies WK52-08-PERF principle (no per-frame allocations beyond the dot draws).

In `HUD.render()` skip any `_render_right_panel_overview` call. In `render_coordinator` the second `_render_hero_minimap` call (~line 78-88, the `if now_mode == ViewMode.HERO_FOCUS` branch that draws into the right rect) must be skipped when right rect width is 0. Also remove the right panel `Tab: Panel` hint cache rendering, the right_panel_visible toggle keypress effect (leave the attribute but it's a no-op), and the `_panel_hint_cache` blit. Remove the right-panel hover hit region from `virtual_pointer_in_hud_chrome`. The Recall and Memorial buttons live in the BOTTOM bar, not the right panel — they stay.

D5. CHATPANEL RELOCATES TO LOWER SECTION OF LEFT COLUMN; MINIMAP SHRINKS.

    - Chat rect: sits between minimap.bottom and bottom_bar.top, hugging x=0, width = LEFT_COL_W. Compute as `chat_rect = pygame.Rect(0, minimap.bottom, LEFT_COL_W, bottom.y - minimap.bottom)` (clamped to >=0 height).

After R4 the minimap top is higher up so a chat rect can fit between minimap.bottom and bottom.top. Actually re-anchor: with shorter minimap and chat below, minimap.y = bottom.y - chat_h - minimap_size. Pick `chat_h = 140` and `minimap_size = 180` giving total 320 above bottom bar. Confirm bottom bar y unchanged.
  Chat rendering:
    - In `game/ui/chat_panel.py` (or its caller in HUD), accept a target rect parameter and render into it. If currently the chat reads from `self.side_panel_width`, replace with chat_rect.width.
    - Remove the chat from any right-panel render path.
    - In `HUD.render()`, after minimap and watch card, call `self._chat_panel.render(surface, chat_rect, game_state)`.
    - The chat must remain functional — typing, history scroll, message wrapping. If existing tests break, fix them to use the new rect.

Does it short-circuit if rect.height < N? READ FIRST:

  - The R5 receipt at `.cursor/plans/agent_logs/agent_08_UX_UI_Director.json` -> sprints["wk52_attachment_phase3_radar_alerts_memorial"].rounds["wk52_r5_corner_layout"] for context — D1 wired ChatPanel.render / render_idle_dock into _render_watch_card_chrome and stored _watch_card_chat_rect.

  - `game/ui/pin_slot.py` `pin()` method — add the auto-expand effect here, OR in the HUD's pin handler.
  - the call site that invokes pin_slot.pin() — grep for it; HUD-side detection of the pin transition is preferred so 08 stays within owned paths.

DELIVERABLES (both required for status=done):

D1. CHAT BAND ACTUALLY RENDERS CONTENT.

  Repro: in screenshot 5 the chat rect inside the hero card is the right size and position (lower ~96px of the watch card, hugging x=0 and the card's bottom edge), but there is NO visible chat UI inside it — no input prompt, no placeholder text, no message history scroll area, just a flat black fill. The chat panel render call exists but produces no visible content.

  Investigate AND fix the actual cause. Likely candidates:

    (a) `ChatPanel.render_idle_dock` only paints a background and is not the right method for an active chat affordance — the in-card call should use `ChatPanel.render` (the full render path used when chat was in the right panel) with the in-card rect.
    (b) The full `ChatPanel.render` requires args (game_state, input_text, history list, font, theme, hud, etc.) that the in-card call site is not passing.
    (c) The chat panel internally short-circuits when `rect.height < N` or when `provider is None` or when there is no game_state['chat_messages']. Inspect for early returns.
    (d) The chat panel renders to an internal cached surface that was sized for the old right-panel rect; the cache is never invalidated to the new in-card rect.

  Required behavior after the fix (acceptance):
    - Chat band in the expanded card shows: a thin separator divider at the top, then a scrollable message-history area (empty in screenshots), then an input affordance at the bottom (visible cursor / placeholder text like 'Type to chat…' or '> _' is fine). The placeholder text MUST be visible in screenshots even when no messages have been exchanged.
    - Chat does NOT render when card is minimized (already correct from R5).
    - Clicking inside the chat rect should focus the input (existing logic — preserve it). Just make sure the click region matches `_watch_card_chat_rect` and the keyboard focus path still works.
    - Add a regression test in tests/test_wk52_watch_card.py that constructs a HUD with a pinned hero and an expanded card, calls render() once, and asserts the in-card chat region has at least one non-background pixel (i.e., something was drawn beyond the band's background fill). Use surface.get_at() inside the chat rect on a row offset where placeholder text would appear.

D2. WATCH CARD AUTO-EXPANDS ON FIRST PIN.

  Today `_watch_card_expanded` is False at HUD construction and stays False until the user clicks the chevron. The user wants: as soon as a hero gets pinned (pin_slot transitions from no hero to a hero_id), the watch card should default to EXPANDED so the player sees the full info immediately. The chevron remains interactive — the user can manually collapse afterwards.

  Implementation:
    - In `game/ui/pin_slot.py` `pin()` method: after setting `self.hero_id`, raise a small flag like `self._just_pinned = True` (or expose a callback / event). Do NOT directly couple pin_slot to HUD.
    - In `game/ui/hud.py`: in `render()` or in the watch card layout step, detect the pin transition (e.g., compare current pin_slot.hero_id to a HUD-side `_last_pinned_hero_id`); when it transitions from None to a hero_id (or from one hero to a different hero_id), set `self._watch_card_expanded = True`. Persist `_last_pinned_hero_id` on HUD for the next-frame compare.
    - When the user manually toggles the chevron AFTER auto-expand, that takes precedence — don't snap back to expanded on subsequent frames just because the pin is still active. The auto-expand fires only on the pin transition edge, not every frame.
    - Unpinning (transition to no hero_id) should NOT force-collapse — let the existing 'card disappears when no pin' logic handle it (or if you prefer, reset `_last_pinned_hero_id` so the next pin re-fires the auto-expand).
    - Add a regression test: simulate pin on a fresh HUD with `_watch_card_expanded=False`; render once; assert `_watch_card_expanded == True`. Then simulate a chevron click to collapse; render again; assert `_watch_card_expanded == False` (auto-expand does not snap back). Then unpin and re-pin a different hero; assert auto-expand fires again.

DELIVERABLE D2 (refactor — extract shared InfoCard):

  Goal: a single source of truth for the card chrome that both the hero watch card and the building summary use. NO visual change for the hero card after this deliverable — pure refactor, all R5/R6/R7 tests still pass.

  Implementation:
    - New module game/ui/info_card.py with class InfoCard. Responsibilities:
        1. Draw the card chrome: rounded-rect background, border, header strip with optional chevron.
        2. Lay out and render a sequence of body sections passed in by the caller. A section is a small dataclass (or callable + height) that knows how to render into a sub-rect inside the body.
        3. Provide hit-test helpers: header rect (for chevron), body sub-rects by section id.
        4. Handle the edge-anchoring (flush left, sits directly above the corner minimap) and the auto-clamp from R5 (_effective_watch_card_h equivalent — generalize so any caller passes a min_top).
    - Section types you'll need:
        HeaderSection(name: str, expanded: bool)  -> renders header bar + chevron, fixed height
        HeroMapSection(rect_attr_name: str)        -> reserves the rect for render_coordinator to blit into; stores the rect on HUD under the given attribute (e.g., 'watch_card_map_rect')
        StatsGridSection(rows: list[StatsRow])    -> 2x2 grid (HP|Mana, XP|Lvl with Chat button when chat hidden)
        ChatBandSection(...)                       -> delegates to chat_panel.render_watch_band
        BuildingStatsSection(building)             -> HP bar + status text + queue/progress (use existing _render_building_summary logic, just inside this section)
